summary: Added a new decompilation of `src/dolphin/dvd/dvdlow.c`, reaching a reported 97.10% file match for the Dolphin DVD low-level layer. The PR implemented many weak DVDLow entry points, interrupt/alarm handling, DI/PI register interaction, workaround read scheduling, and updated related DVD/OS headers and platform macros needed for correct typing and section/weak attributes.
what_changed: Created `src/dolphin/dvd/dvdlow.c` with 459 lines implementing the low-level DVD interface and matching report coverage of 97.10% for a 3.67 KB file.

Implemented or declared weak low-level DVD functions including `__DVDInitWA`, `__DVDInterruptHandler`, `DVDLowRead`, `DVDLowSeek`, `DVDLowReadDiskID`, `DVDLowStopMotor`, `DVDLowRequestError`, `DVDLowInquiry`, `DVDLowAudioStream`, `DVDLowRequestAudioStatus`, `DVDLowAudioBufferConfig`, `DVDLowReset`, `DVDLowBreak`, and `DVDLowClearCallback`.
Added internal DVD read workaround state and command sequencing via `CommandList`, `NextCommandNumber`, `ProcessNextCommand`, `SeekTwiceBeforeRead`, `WaitBeforeRead`, and `DoJustRead`.
Added timeout and workaround alarm handling with `AlarmForWA`, `AlarmForTimeout`, `AlarmForBreak`, `AlarmHandler`, `AlarmHandlerForTimeout`, and `SetTimeoutAlarm`.
Implemented direct DI/PI hardware register programming through `__DIRegs` and `__PIRegs` for DVD commands, interrupt cause handling, reset timing, and transfer setup.
Updated `dvdlow.h` prototypes from `void` returns to `bool` for most DVDLow command functions and gave parameters concrete types such as `DVDDiskID*`, `DVDDriveInfo*`, and `bool enable`.
Changed `DVDLowReadDiskID` to take `DVDDiskID*`; `dvd.c` now casts `tmpBuffer` to `DVDDiskID*` with an explicit `@todo Incorrect cast` note.
Named the previously anonymous DVD buffer struct as `DVDBuffer` and changed `bootFilePosition` from `u32` to `void*` in `dvd.h`.
Moved `SDATA` definition out of `placeholder.h` into `Runtime/platform.h` and added a `WEAK` macro using `__declspec(weak)` for MWERKS builds outside `M2CTX`.
Removed the old `MWERKS_GNUC` macro block from `Runtime/platform.h`.
Centralized `__PIRegs` declaration in `OSInit.h`, added `__DIRegs[16]`, and removed duplicate/local `__PIRegs` extern declarations from OS files.
Added `-Wno-undefined-internal` to `compile_flags.txt`, likely to tolerate internal declarations emitted by this decompilation pattern.
smart_moves: Used `WEAK` declarations for SDK-style low-level DVD functions, matching the likely library linkage model while keeping M2CTX behavior clean through an empty macro outside MWERKS.
Centralized hardware register externs in `OSInit.h` instead of duplicating `__PIRegs` declarations across OS source files, reducing drift in register array sizes and addresses.
Improved header prototypes to carry actual return values and parameter types, which makes call sites and future matching work less dependent on casts or placeholder signatures.
Kept known type uncertainty visible by marking the `DVDLowReadDiskID((DVDDiskID*) &tmpBuffer, ...)` call with `@todo Incorrect cast` instead of silently hiding the mismatch.
Modeled asynchronous DVD command flow explicitly with small command queue state and alarm callbacks, which helps preserve control-flow shape for matching `DVDLowRead` and interrupt behavior.
Relocated section/linkage helper macros from `placeholder.h` to `Runtime/platform.h`, making `SDATA` and `WEAK` generally available rather than tied to placeholder infrastructure.
decomp_lessons: For Dolphin SDK low-level modules, matching often requires preserving exact global state layout: booleans, volatile fields, command counters, alarms, callback globals, and previous/current transfer metadata all affect codegen.
Weak SDK symbols can be represented with a reusable `WEAK` macro gated on `__MWERKS__` and `!M2CTX`; this avoids scattering compiler-specific attributes through placeholder headers.
Hardware register externs should live in common OS headers with precise array sizes and `AT_ADDRESS` addresses so DVD, interrupt, and reset modules share the same declarations.

When prototypes are corrected from placeholders, update both headers and call sites together; return-type changes from `void` to `bool` can be required for matching even when callers ignore the result.
Keep casts that are known to be structurally suspicious annotated with TODOs. This preserves buildability while flagging type work for future cleanup.
Alarm and interrupt handlers may need temporary `OSContext` manipulation (`OSClearContext`, `OSSetCurrentContext`) to match SDK exception-callback behavior.
DVD DI command functions follow a repeated register-writing pattern: set `Callback`, clear `StopAtNextInt`, populate `__DIRegs[2..7]`, then install a timeout alarm.
naming_conventions: SDK internal functions use double-underscore prefixes such as `__DVDInitWA`, `__DVDInterruptHandler`, and `__DVDLowSetWAType`.
Low-level DVD public functions use `DVDLow` prefixes, e.g. `DVDLowRead`, `DVDLowSeek`, `DVDLowReset`, and `DVDLowClearCallback`.

Global static DVDLow state uses PascalCase-like names from SDK style: `FirstRead`, `StopAtNextInt`, `LastLength`, `Callback`, `ResetOccurred`, `WaitingCoverClose`, `WorkAroundType`, and `NextCommandNumber`.
Alarm globals are named for purpose: `AlarmForWA`, `AlarmForTimeout`, and `AlarmForBreak`.
Hardware register arrays follow OS SDK names `__DIRegs` and `__PIRegs` and are declared with `AT_ADDRESS`.
Concrete struct names should replace anonymous placeholder structs when the SDK type is known or useful, as with `typedef struct DVDBuffer { ... } DVDBuffer;`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced `WEAK` as `__declspec(weak)` for MWERKS builds to match weak SDK function linkage while compiling away under non-MWERKS or M2CTX.
Introduced `SDATA` as `__declspec(section ".sdata")` in the platform layer, preserving small-data placement when needed by matching code.
Added `-Wno-undefined-internal` to suppress warnings introduced by matching-oriented internal/weak declarations.

Used `volatile` on interrupt-shared state such as `StopAtNextInt`, `LastResetEnd`, `ResetOccurred`, `WaitingCoverClose`, `LastReadFinished`, `LastCommandWasRead`, and `NextCommandNumber` to model hardware/interrupt interactions and influence codegen.
Used explicit casts from pointers to `u32` for DI DMA address registers, e.g. assigning `(u32) addr`, `(u32) diskID`, and `(u32) info` into `__DIRegs[5]`.
Preserved direct bitmask manipulation for interrupt cause decoding from `__DIRegs[0]` and cover/reset handling through `__DIRegs[1]`.
Preserved busy-wait reset timing in `DVDLowReset` using `__OSGetSystemTime()` and `OSMicrosecondsToTicks(12)`.
The PR body reported many functions at 100% match, with remaining mismatch concentrated in `__DVDInterruptHandler` at 91.01%, `DVDLowRead` at 94.27%, and `Read` at 99.93%.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The diff itself includes an inline `@todo Incorrect cast` on the `DVDLowReadDiskID((DVDDiskID*) &tmpBuffer, cbForStateCoverClosed)` call, documenting unresolved type uncertainty.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching `__DVDInterruptHandler`, `DVDLowRead`, or `Read` in `src/dolphin/dvd/dvdlow.c`.
Search for future cleanup of the `@todo Incorrect cast` around `DVDLowReadDiskID` and `tmpBuffer`.
Check whether `DVDBuffer.bootFilePosition` remains `void*` or is refined in later DVD type cleanup.
Search for other PRs that reuse or modify the `WEAK` and `SDATA` macros in `Runtime/platform.h`.
Look for follow-up changes to `__DIRegs` and `__PIRegs` declarations or register array sizes in OS headers.
